items.title: The development of aperiodic neural activity in the human brain
items.author: Elizabeth L. Johnson
items.content: This study establishes how aperiodic activity, a ubiquitous signal linked to neural noise, develops in localized brain regions and illuminates the development of prefrontal control during adolescence in the development of attention and memory.

items.title: Speech sequencing in the human precentral gyrus
items.author: Edward F. Chang
items.content: Liu et al. examine the role of sustained neural activity in the planning and production of speech sequences, revealing a key role for the middle precentral gyrus.

items.title: Governments should prioritize well-being over economic growth
items.author: Kelsey J. O’Connor
items.content: Despite its prominence in public discourse, economic growth does not translate into lasting improvements in well-being. To improve people’s lives, policymakers should shift their focus from economic growth to well-being. We provide example policies that could foster thriving, sustainable and inclusive societies.

items.title: Advancing the psychology of social class with large-scale replications in four countries
items.author: Frédérique Autin
items.content: Social class is assumed to be associated with many different individual outcomes and behaviours. This Registered Report aimed to replicate 35 key hypotheses from 17 correlational and 5 experimental studies using quota-based or probability samples.

items.title: Unattended working memory items are coded by persistent activity in human medial temporal lobe neurons
items.author: J. Kamiński
items.content: Paluch et al. show that unattended working memory items, as well as attended ones, are encoded in persistent activity in the medial temporal lobe.

items.title: User control of search algorithms would improve science
items.author: Paul E. Smaldino
items.content: User control of search algorithms would improve science

items.title: A unified framework integrating psychology and geography
items.author: Douglas T. Kenrick
items.content: In this Perspective, Götz et al. propose the unifying Geographical–Psychological Interactionist Framework, which aims to integrate psychology and geography to account for the context in which human behaviour takes place.

items.title: Machine learning-assisted optimization of dietary intervention against dementia risk
items.author: Jin-Tai Yu
items.content: Chen et al. use UK Biobank data to reveal a dietary pattern associated with a lower risk of dementia, and validate this in additional cohorts.
